userMessage:
here is teh datasheet forthe DUT 
1. Set up UART decode on B1 source CH1 115200 baud 
   8N1, trigger on start bit, single sequence

2. Set up I2C decode on B1 clock CH1 data CH2, 
   trigger on address 0x48 write

3. Measure SYSOSC frequency on CH1, add frequency 
   jitter and period measurements

4. Power rail test: CH1 VDD 3.3V, CH2 VREF, 
   measure noise floor on both rails

5. Capture SPI transaction: clock CH1, MOSI CH2, 
   CS CH3 active low

you can group each test

warnings: Detected measurement setup/result scatter; grouped into Add Measurements and Read Results.

response.text:
I replaced the placeholder with a grouped UART/I2C/SYSOSC/power-rails/SPI flow using verified scope/bus/measurement SCPI where available, and inserted comment placeholders where the specific SCPI trigger/rail/SPI-source details were not verified.

actionsJson.summary: Replace placeholder flow with a grouped UART/I2C/SYSOSC/power-rails/SPI setup and measurement flow (with manual placeholders for any unverified SCPI details).
findings: Verified bus type + RS232C UART source/bitrate commands: BUS:B1:TYPe RS232C, BUS:B1:RS232C:SOUrce CH1, BUS:B1:RS232C:BITRate:CUSTom 115200.
Verified I2C trigger-related commands: TRIGger:A:BUS:B1:I2C:DATa:DIRection WRITE, TRIGger:A:BUS:B1:I2C:ADDRess:MODe ADDR7, TRIGger:A:BUS:B1:I2C:ADDRess:VALue <quoted string>.
Verified measurement framework: MEASUrement:ADDMEAS FREQUENCY/DJ/PERIOD, and result query format MEASUrement:MEASx:RESUlts:CURRentacq:MEAN?.

Not verified in this turn: UART trigger on start-bit condition SCPI, I2C trigger condition token (e.g., ADDRESS vs ADDRESSANDDATA) and I2C clock/data source mapping, power-rail noise-floor measurement SCPI and how to select VDD/VREF, SPI decode source mapping (clock/MOSI/CS) and CS active-low setting, and any SPI trigger/condition SCPI.
suggestedFixes: For UART: add the specific TRIGger:{A|B}:BUS:B1:RS232C:CONDition command for “start bit”.
For I2C: set the trigger condition token/qualifier command (ADDRESS/ADDRESSANDDATA etc) plus clock/data source mapping for BUS:B1:I2C (CH1/CH2).
For power rails: implement the exact SCPI measurement(s) that correspond to ‘noise floor’ and define how ‘VDD 3.3V’ and ‘VREF’ are mapped to channels/scalar inputs.
For SPI: configure BUS:B1:TYPE SPI plus the verified clock/MOSI/CS source commands and CS polarity (active low), and optionally any trigger condition.
Auto-repaired missing/duplicate step ids in replace_flow actions for apply compatibility.
Measurement steps were regrouped into Add Measurements and Read Results.
Filled connect/disconnect instrumentIds with workspace alias "MSO6B" for direct chat apply compatibility.

flow.name: UART/I2C/SYSOSC/Power/SPI Decode & Measurements
flow.description: Grouped UART, I2C, SYSOSC frequency+jitter+period measurements, power rail noise-floor placeholders, and SPI transaction capture placeholders.

steps.label: Test 1: UART decode (B1, CH1 @ 115200 8N1, trigger start bit, single sequence)

params.text: Placeholder: set UART format (8N1) and the UART trigger condition on START BIT (exact TRIGger:{A|B}:BUS:B<x>:RS232C:CONDition command not verified this turn).

steps.label: Test 2: I2C decode (B1 clock CH1, data CH2, trigger address 0x48 write)

children.label: I2C source mapping to verify manually

params.text: Placeholder: configure I2C clock/data sources for BUS:B1:I2C (clock=CH1, data=CH2) using the specific verified BUS:B1:I2C:*:SOUrce commands (not verified this turn).

children.label: I2C trigger value encoding to verify manually

params.text: The command TRIGger:A:BUS:B1:I2C:ADDRess:VALue expects a quoted binary string (not verified whether hex is accepted). Attempted below with binary for 0x48 as "01001000"; adjust if your fixture uses a different address-length/bit alignment.

children.label: I2C trigger condition token to verify manually

params.text: Placeholder: set the I2C trigger CONDition to the correct token that makes use of ADDRESS (e.g., ADDRESS or ADDRESSANDDATA). Exact TRIGger:{A|B}:BUS:B<x>:I2C:CONDition command not verified this turn.

steps.label: Test 3: Measure SYSOSC frequency + jitter + period (on CH1)

params.command: MEASUrement:ADDMEAS FREQUENCY; MEASUrement:ADDMEAS DJ; MEASUrement:ADDMEAS PERIOD
MEASUrement:MEAS1:SOUrce1 CH1; MEASUrement:MEAS2:SOUrce1 CH1; MEASUrement:MEAS3:SOUrce1 CH1

steps.label: Test 4: Power rails noise floor (CH1 VDD 3.3V, CH2 VREF)

children.label: Power rail noise-floor SCPI not verified

params.text: Placeholder: add the exact Tektronix/DSA measurement(s) for ‘noise floor’, and configure the VDD (CH1, 3.3V) and VREF (CH2) channel/scalar mapping. The specific verified SCPI for noise-floor measurement was not found this turn.

steps.label: Test 5: Capture SPI transaction (clock CH1, MOSI CH2, CS CH3 active low)

children.label: SPI source mapping & CS polarity to verify manually

params.text: Placeholder: configure BUS:B1:SPI clock/source (CH1), MOSI/source (CH2), CS/source (CH3) and set CS polarity to active low using the specific verified BUS:B1:SPI:* commands (not verified this turn).

children.label: SPI trigger condition to verify manually

params.text: Optional placeholder: set a SPI trigger condition to capture the desired transaction (exact TRIGger:{A|B}:BUS:B<x>:SPI:CONDition and related parameters not verified this turn).
